metadata.position_exact: EFG Graduate Programme 2026 - Corporate Functions - Geneva
metadata.job_summary: 18-month rotational graduate programme at EFG Bank in Geneva starting September 2026. Includes a 6-month rotation in preferred corporate function plus two additional 6-month rotations across business units. Covers areas like Strategic Planning, Compliance, Risk, Finance, and Business Development with continuous learning and mentorship.

research.company_mission: Empowering entrepreneurial minds to create value – today and for the future. Bringing entrepreneurial thinking to Swiss private banking.
research.recent_news: Presented 2026-2028 strategic plan with CHF 130M technology investment for AI-driven solutions to augment CROs, targeting 15% annual profit growth and CHF 70-80M cost reductions through Simplicity initiatives.

resume.professional_summary: Finance graduate with M&A and financial planning experience seeking to join the EFG Graduate Programme - Corporate Functions in Geneva. Proven analytical and problem-solving skills through deal execution at a boutique advisory, financial modeling for EUR 50M mandates, and dashboard automation. International mindset developed through cross-border transactions spanning Europe, Asia, and the Americas.
auraia_bullets: Built financial models (LBO, DCF, comps, precedent transactions) for deals valued at EUR 5M-50M and prepared client presentations for Board meetings and investor pitches requiring strategic insights and analytical problem-solving skills

Staffed on 7 live mandates spanning sell-side advisory, buy-side support, and debt advisory across healthcare, technology, and life sciences with deal values ranging from EUR 5M to EUR 50M in cross-border transactions

Led production of marketing materials and information memoranda, maintained buyer universes of 100+ strategic acquirers, and coordinated data room management across concurrent mandates to ensure seamless transaction execution
resume.rc_bullet: Worked with the CEO of Healthcare and Retail divisions on evaluating acquisition targets in France and Belgium, conducting due diligence on targets with revenues of EUR 10M-30M and preparing investment memos with market analysis, competitive positioning, and financial projections
resume.europ_bullet: Built automated financial dashboards in Excel and PowerBI to track KPIs across 5 business units, performed variance analysis on monthly P&L results for a CHF 50M+ subsidiary, and supported the CFO on budget planning and quarterly forecasting
leadership_bullets: Finalist - McKinsey & Company Business Case - Presented strategic project to Keyrock management showcasing CHF 200K potential annual revenue through predictive modeling
Associate - AIESEC Student Club - Led post-COVID recovery by closing 3 corporate partnerships generating CHF 60K annual revenue and increasing volunteer placements by 40%
Founder - Screeny.ai - Built AI software for investment banks to identify strategic acquirers, adopted by 3 firms and reducing buyer search time by 90%
resume.courses: Corporate Finance, Corporate Governance and M&A, Risk Management, Valuation
resume.skills: Financial Modeling | Due Diligence | Strategic Planning | Data Analysis | PowerBI

cover_letter.subject: **Subject:** Application for Graduate Programme - Corporate Functions Position
cover_letter.intro: I am writing to apply for the EFG Graduate Programme 2026 - Corporate Functions in Geneva. EFG's recently announced 2026-2028 strategic plan, particularly the commitment to invest CHF 130 million in technology to augment CROs with AI-driven solutions, resonates deeply with my experience building Screeny.ai, an AI tool that reduced buyer research time by 90% for M&A advisors. The programme's rotational structure across Strategic Planning, Compliance, Risk, and Finance aligns perfectly with my analytical background and eagerness to gain comprehensive exposure to private banking operations.
cover_letter.body_1: **Strategic Thinking and Analytical Rigor** - My M&A experience at Auraïa Capital required building financial models and coordinating due diligence across 7 concurrent mandates valued at EUR 5M-50M. Working in a lean 3-person team, I developed the organisational skills and forward-thinking mindset to suggest process improvements, from automating buyer research workflows to streamlining data room management. This proactive approach to efficiency directly aligns with EFG's emphasis on commercial excellence and Simplicity initiatives targeting CHF 70-80M in cost reductions.
cover_letter.body_2: **Client-Oriented Approach** - Managing relationships with CEOs, Board members, and advisors throughout transaction lifecycles taught me the importance of tailored advice and partnership-oriented thinking. At Europ Assistance, I supported the CFO on strategic projects for a CHF 50M+ subsidiary, preparing Board presentations and developing profitability models. These experiences mirror EFG's core values of being accountable, hands-on, and solution-driven, which I witnessed firsthand in collaborative environments requiring adaptability and intellectual curiosity.
cover_letter.body_3: **International Mindset and Entrepreneurial Spirit** - Conducting cross-border M&A across Europe, Asia, and the Americas developed my ability to navigate diverse business cultures and regulatory environments. My entrepreneurial background, from founding Screeny.ai to closing CHF 60K in corporate partnerships at AIESEC, demonstrates the self-starter mentality and commitment to excellence that EFG values. The Graduate Programme's rotational model offers the ideal platform to channel this entrepreneurial thinking into private banking innovation.
cover_letter.closing: I am confident that my analytical capabilities, client-oriented approach, and international experience make me a strong fit for the EFG Graduate Programme. I am excited about the opportunity to contribute to EFG's mission of empowering entrepreneurial minds while developing expertise across corporate functions. I look forward to discussing how my background aligns with the programme's objectives.

email.subject: Application - Graduate Programme - Corporate Functions - Adrian Turion
email.body:
Dear Hiring Team,

Please find attached my resume and cover letter for the EFG Graduate Programme 2026 - Corporate Functions in Geneva.

EFG's commitment to invest CHF 130 million in AI-driven solutions to augment Client Relationship Officers resonates with my experience building Screeny.ai, a tool that reduced M&A buyer research time by 90%. I am eager to bring this innovation mindset to your rotational programme.

I would welcome the opportunity to discuss my application further.

Best regards,
Adrian Turion
+41 77 262 37 96
turionadrian@gmail.com
